message:
fix(chordal): port NetworkX MCS-M into complete_to_chordal_graph

Previous implementation all-pairs-filled the direct neighborhood of z and
never took the path-through-lower-weight chord detection step, producing
27 chord fill-ins on wheel_graph(10) vs NetworkX's 6. It also ran the
full loop on already-chordal inputs instead of returning zero-alpha
early. Rewritten to match networkx/algorithms/chordal.py line-for-line
per Berry/Blair/Heggernes/Peyton 2004. Adds parity tests that assert
chord-count matches NetworkX for wheel/cycle/ladder non-chordal cases
and that path_graph/complete_graph/star/empty inputs short-circuit to
all-zero alpha. Closes franken_networkx-07j7.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"franken_networkx-049","title":"BUG: k_edge_augmentation returns [] for k>=2 — implement full algorithm","description":"## Problem
k_edge_augmentation() at __init__.py:6536-6543 only handles k=1 (connecting components via one edge each). For k>=2 it unconditionally returns [], which is WRONG — it should find edges to add to make the graph k-edge-connected.

## NetworkX behavior
nx.k_edge_augmentation(G, k) yields edges (u,v) that, when added, make G k-edge-connected. Uses:
- k=1: connect components optimally
- k=2: bridge augmentation (find bridges, add parallel paths)
- k>=3: greedy partial augmentation via repeated min-cut

Supports: avail (restricted edge set), weight (minimize total weight), partial (best-effort).

## Fix plan
1. k=1: current impl works but could be improved (use spanning forest for optimality)
2. k=2: implement bridge-based augmentation — find all bridges, for each bridge find an alternative path or add a new edge
3. k>=3: implement greedy — repeatedly find min edge cut < k, add edge across cut
4. Support avail parameter (restricted edge set)
5. Support weight parameter (minimize total added weight)
6. Support partial flag (return what we can even if full k-connectivity not achievable)

## Testing requirements
Create tests/python/test_k_edge_augmentation_parity.py:
- test_k1_disconnected: G with 3 components, verify augmentation connects them
- test_k1_already_connected: verify returns empty
- test_k2_with_bridges: graph with bridges, verify bridges eliminated after augmentation
- test_k2_matches_nx: compare edge set against nx.k_edge_augmentation(G, 2)
- test_k3_matches_nx: compare against nx.k_edge_augmentation(G, 3)
- test_avail_parameter: only use edges from avail set
- test_weight_parameter: verify total weight is minimized
- test_partial_flag: returns best-effort when full augmentation impossible
- test_augmented_graph_is_k_connected: add returned edges, verify edge_connectivity >= k
- E2E: generate random graph, augment for k=1..4, verify connectivity after each

Run: pytest tests/python/test_k_edge_augmentation_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs: add k_edge_augmentation Rust impl
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs: add PyO3 binding
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewire to Rust
- tests/python/test_k_edge_augmentation_parity.py: new test file (10+ tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:16:35.675285907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:44:25.802036968Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:44:25.801771054Z","close_reason":"Fixed k_edge_augmentation: k=1 uses native component-connection, k>=2 delegates to NetworkX (which has optimized bridge augmentation for k=2 and greedy partial augmentation for k>=3). Previously returned [] for all k>=2. Added 8 parity tests in test_k_edge_augmentation_parity.py covering k=0,1,2,3, already-connected, bridge elimination, and generic connectivity verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-06yo","title":"NX-REPLACE: Attribute access — get/set_node_attributes, get/set_edge_attributes","description":"## Functions (4) — pure Python rewrites, no Rust needed
1. set_node_attributes (__init__.py:1102) — sets attrs on multiple nodes from dict/value
2. get_node_attributes (__init__.py:1126) — returns {node: attr_value} dict
3. set_edge_attributes (__init__.py:1147) — sets attrs on multiple edges from dict/value
4. get_edge_attributes (__init__.py:1170) — returns {edge: attr_value} dict

## Why high priority
Most frequently called utility functions. Every workflow that reads/writes metadata uses them. The _to_nx() round-trip is especially wasteful since attrs already live in Python-side dicts.

## Implementation plan
Pure Python, operating directly on FNX graph's attribute storage:
1. get_node_attributes(G, name): {n: G.nodes[n][name] for n in G.nodes() if name in G.nodes[n]}
2. set_node_attributes(G, values, name=None): if dict-of-dicts, G.nodes[n].update(d); if dict+name, G.nodes[n][name]=v; if scalar+name, set for all nodes
3. get_edge_attributes: same pattern for edges
4. set_edge_attributes: same pattern for edges
Must handle: default parameter, dict-of-values vs dict-of-dicts, scalar broadcast.

## Testing requirements
Create tests/python/test_attribute_access_parity.py:
- test_set_node_attrs_dict_with_name: {0:'red',1:'blue'} + name='color'
- test_set_node_attrs_dict_of_dicts: {0:{'color':'red','size':5}}
- test_set_node_attrs_scalar_broadcast: value='default' to all nodes
- test_get_node_attrs_basic: verify returns correct dict
- test_get_node_attrs_default: verify default param for missing attrs
- test_get_node_attrs_missing_attr: verify nodes without attr are excluded
- test_set_edge_attrs_dict_with_name: set weight on specific edges
- test_set_edge_attrs_scalar: broadcast value to all edges
- test_get_edge_attrs_basic: verify correct dict
- test_get_edge_attrs_default: verify default for missing
- test_roundtrip: set then get returns same values
- test_matches_nx_on_complex_graph: 20-node graph, verify against NX

Run: pytest tests/python/test_attribute_access_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewrite 4 functions
- tests/python/test_attribute_access_parity.py: new test file (12+ tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:22.926030354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T13:54:47.146747100Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T13:54:47.146228128Z","close_reason":"Already complete — all 4 attribute functions are native Python with no _to_nx delegation. set_node_attributes, get_node_attributes, set_edge_attributes, get_edge_attributes all operate directly on FNX graph's attribute dicts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-072","title":"Add coloring strategies and chordal graph detection","description":"Coloring is at ~10% parity (only greedy_color). Add: strategy parameter support for greedy_color (largest_first, random_sequential, smallest_last, DSATUR), is_chordal, chordal_graph_treewidth. Also add graph_number_of_cliques for clique counting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-21T08:02:55.199784823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:12:14.561826885Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:12:14.561043081Z","close_reason":"Added greedy_color strategy parameter (largest_first, smallest_last, random_sequential, DSATUR/saturation_largest_first). Coloring coverage improved from ~10% to ~40%.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-07j7","title":"Port NetworkX MCS-M into complete_to_chordal_graph","description":"Current franken_networkx complete_to_chordal_graph deviates from NetworkX MCS-M. Symptoms: wheel_graph(10) gets 27 fill-in chords vs 6 from nx; already-chordal inputs (path_graph, complete_graph) return alpha values set by the full loop instead of all-zero early-return; does all-pairs fill-in on direct neighbors instead of MCS-M path-through-lower-weight chord detection. Port networkx/algorithms/chordal.py:complete_to_chordal_graph verbatim (including the 'if nx.is_chordal(H): return H, {node:0}' early return and the chords-set deferred-add). Add parity tests covering wheel_graph(10), cycle_graph(6), and already-chordal inputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"claude-fnx-port","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:31:15.529081814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T21:32:48.957422618Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T21:32:48.954871139Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-0c76","title":"[A3] Promote or delete the ~75 ad-hoc root-level test scripts","description":"**Track:** A. **Priority:** P1. **Type:** task.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:26.167651233Z","updated_at":"2026-04-15T23:16:54.829047529Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T23:16:54.826283888Z","close_reason":"scripts already deleted as part of A1 root cleanup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-0c76","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-9yad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:39.002995098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-0c76","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-pak0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:38.798265113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-0e2f","title":"[D2m] Preserve mode through graph operator transforms","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-16T04:34:27.743757989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T04:37:10.017972015Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T04:37:10.015104373Z","close_reason":"Preserved hardened mode through graph operator transforms; validated with rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -13891,34 +13891,37 @@ def add_children(parent, children_):
 def complete_to_chordal_graph(G):
     """Return a chordal completion and elimination ordering map.
 
-    Uses MCS-M (maximum cardinality search with minimal fill-in) to produce
-    a chordal supergraph and a perfect elimination ordering.
+    Uses MCS-M (maximum cardinality search with minimal fill-in) per Berry,
+    Blair, Heggernes, Peyton (2004) to produce a chordal supergraph H and an
+    elimination ordering alpha. For inputs that are already chordal, returns
+    H equal to G and alpha mapping every node to 0.
     """
     H = G.copy()
-    nodes = list(G.nodes())
-    n = len(nodes)
-    weight = {v: 0 for v in nodes}
-    alpha = {}
-    unnumbered = set(nodes)
-
-    for i in range(n, 0, -1):
-        # Pick unnumbered node with maximum weight.
-        z = max(unnumbered, key=lambda v: weight[v])
+    alpha = {node: 0 for node in H}
+    if is_chordal(H):
+        return H, alpha
+    chords = set()
+    weight = {node: 0 for node in H.nodes()}
+    unnumbered_nodes = list(H.nodes())
+    for i in range(len(H.nodes()), 0, -1):
+        z = max(unnumbered_nodes, key=lambda node: weight[node])
+        unnumbered_nodes.remove(z)
         alpha[z] = i
-        unnumbered.remove(z)
-        # Update weights and add fill edges.
-        update_nodes = set()
-        for y in unnumbered:
-            if H.has_edge(z, y):
-                update_nodes.add(y)
-        for y in update_nodes:
-            weight[y] += 1
-            # Add fill edges between y and other numbered neighbors of z
-            # that are also neighbors of y's reach through z.
-            for w in update_nodes:
-                if w != y and not H.has_edge(y, w):
-                    H.add_edge(y, w)
-
+        update_nodes = []
+        for y in unnumbered_nodes:
+            if G.has_edge(y, z):
+                update_nodes.append(y)
+            else:
+                y_weight = weight[y]
+                lower_nodes = [
+                    node for node in unnumbered_nodes if weight[node] < y_weight
+                ]
+                if has_path(H.subgraph(lower_nodes + [z, y]), y, z):
+                    update_nodes.append(y)
+                    chords.add((z, y))
+        for node in update_nodes:
+            weight[node] += 1
+    H.add_edges_from(chords)
     return H, alpha

@@ -540,6 +540,57 @@ def test_complete_to_chordal(self):
         assert fnx.is_chordal(H)
 
 
+@needs_nx
+class TestCompleteToChordalGraphParity:
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
+        "builder",
+        [
+            lambda: nx.wheel_graph(10),
+            lambda: nx.cycle_graph(5),
+            lambda: nx.cycle_graph(6),
+            lambda: nx.cycle_graph(8),
+            lambda: nx.ladder_graph(4),
+        ],
+    )
+    def test_non_chordal_matches_networkx_counts(self, builder):
+        Gn = builder()
+        Gf = fnx.Graph()
+        Gf.add_nodes_from(Gn.nodes())
+        Gf.add_edges_from(Gn.edges())
+
+        Hn, alpha_n = nx.complete_to_chordal_graph(Gn)
+        Hf, alpha_f = fnx.complete_to_chordal_graph(Gf)
+
+        nx_added = Hn.number_of_edges() - Gn.number_of_edges()
+        fnx_added = Hf.number_of_edges() - Gf.number_of_edges()
+        assert fnx_added == nx_added, f"chord count {fnx_added} != nx {nx_added}"
+        assert fnx.is_chordal(Hf)
+        assert set(alpha_f.keys()) == set(alpha_n.keys())
+        assert sorted(alpha_f.values()) == sorted(alpha_n.values())
+
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
+        "builder",
+        [
+            lambda: nx.path_graph(5),
+            lambda: nx.complete_graph(4),
+            lambda: nx.star_graph(4),
+            lambda: nx.empty_graph(3),
+        ],
+    )
+    def test_already_chordal_returns_zero_alpha_and_unchanged_graph(self, builder):
+        Gn = builder()
+        Gf = fnx.Graph()
+        Gf.add_nodes_from(Gn.nodes())
+        Gf.add_edges_from(Gn.edges())
+
+        Hf, alpha_f = fnx.complete_to_chordal_graph(Gf)
+
+        assert Hf.number_of_edges() == Gf.number_of_edges()
+        assert set(Hf.nodes()) == set(Gf.nodes())
+        assert all(v == 0 for v in alpha_f.values())
+        assert set(alpha_f.keys()) == set(Gf.nodes())
+
+
 def _build_find_induced_nodes_case(graph, case_name):
     if case_name == "path_0_3":
         graph.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3)])
